title: Ensure all OCI IAM user accounts have a valid and current  email address

input_row.title: Ensure all OCI IAM user accounts have a valid and current  email address
input_row.description: All OCI IAM local user accounts have an email address field associated with the account. It is recommended to specify an email address that is valid and current. If you have an email address in your user profile, you can use the Forgot Password link on the sign on page to have a temporary password sent to you.
input_row.rationale: Having a valid and current email address associated with an OCI IAM local user account allows you to tie the account to identity in your organization. It also allows that user to reset their password if it is forgotten or lost.
input_row.audit: From Console: 1. Login to OCI Console. 2. Select Identity & Security from the Services menu. 3. Select Domains from the Identity menu. 4. For each domain listed, click on the name and select Users. 5. Click on an individual user under the Username heading. 6. Ensure a valid and current email address is next to Email and Recovery email.
input_row.remediation: From Console: 1. Login to OCI Console. 2. Select Identity & Security from the Services menu. 3. Select Domains from the Identity menu. 4. For each domain listed, click on the name and select Users. 5. Click on each non-complaint user. 6. Click on Edit User. 7. Enter a valid and current email address in the Email and Recovery Email text boxes. 8. Click Save Changes
input_row.additional_information: The Audit Procedure and Remediation Procedure for OCI IAM without Identity Domains can be found in the CIS OCI Foundation Benchmark 2.0.0 under the respective recommendations.
input_row.justification: This control is specific to Oracle Cloud Infrastructure (OCI) and involves checking user email attributes within OCI IAM, which is not directly applicable to AWS. AWS IAM does not inherently store user email addresses in a way that can be directly queried or validated through AWS APIs like boto3 or AWS CLI. Therefore, this requires a manual verification process to ensure compliance.
input_row.manual_summary: 1. Log in to the AWS Management Console. 2. Navigate to the IAM service. 3. Select "Users" from the IAM dashboard. 4. For each user, check if there is a tag or custom attribute that stores the user's email address. 5. Verify that the email address is valid and current by checking the format and confirming with organizational records. 6. Document any users without a valid email address and take corrective action to update their profiles.

step1_initial_assessment:
**AUDIT APPROACH:** Manual

---

**MANUAL STEPS:**

1. **Log in to the OCI Console.**
   - Open https://cloud.oracle.com and authenticate with appropriate credentials.

2. **Navigate to Identity & Security → Domains.**
   - In the main menu, select "Identity & Security," then "Domains."

3. **For each Identity Domain:**
   - Click on the domain name.
   - Select "Users" to list all user accounts within that domain.

4. **Review individual users:**
   - For each user under the "Username" heading, click the username to open their profile.

5. **Verify user email fields:**
   - Confirm that an email address appears in the "Email" and "Recovery email" fields.
   - Assess whether the email address appears to be valid (e.g., correct format: name@domain.com) and current according to your organizational records.

6. **Document non-compliance:**
   - Note any users missing an email or with a clearly invalid/obsolete email address.
   - Prepare a list of users who require updates for reporting or remediation.

7. **Remediation (if required):**
   - For each non-compliant user, while in the user detail view, click "Edit User."
   - Enter a valid and current email address into the "Email" and "Recovery Email" fields.
   - Save changes and confirm that these have updated in the user's profile.

---

**SOURCE:** CIS_Oracle_Cloud_Infrastructure_Foundations_Benchmark_v3_0_0
